records.ety: [OE. litel, lutel, AS. ltel, lītel, lt; akin to OS. littil, D. luttel, LG. lütt, OHG. luzzil, MHG. lützel; and perh. to AS. lytig deceitful, lot deceit, Goth. liuts deceitful, lutōn to deceive; cf. also Icel. lītill little, Sw. liten, Dan. liden, lille, Goth. leitils, which appear to have a different root vowel.]

defs: Small in size or extent; not big; diminutive; -- opposed to big or large; as, a little body; a little animal; a little piece of ground; a little hill; a little distance; a little child.
quotes: He sought to see Jesus who he was; and could not for the press, because he was little of stature.

defs: Short in duration; brief; as, a little sleep.
quotes: Best him enough: after a little time, I'll beat him too.

defs: Small in quantity or amount; not much; as, a little food; a little air or water.
quotes: Conceited of their little wisdoms, and doting upon their own fancies.

defs: Small in dignity, power, or importance; not great; insignificant; contemptible.
quotes: When thou wast little in thine own sight, wast thou not made the head of the tribes?

defs: Small in force or efficiency; not strong; weak; slight; inconsiderable; as, little attention or exertion;little effort; little care or diligence.
quotes: By sad experiment I know How little weight my words with thee can find.

defs: Small in extent of views or sympathies; narrow; shallow; contracted; mean; illiberal; ungenerous.
quotes: The long-necked geese of the world that are ever hissing dispraise, Because their natures are little.
The men, and the women, and the little ones.

defs: That which is little; a small quantity, amount, space, or the like.
quotes: There are many expressions, which carrying with them no clear ideas, are like to remove but little of my ignorance.

defs: A small degree or scale; miniature.
quotes: A little, to or in a small degree; to a limited extent; somewhat; for a short time. “ Stay a little.”
The painter flattered her a little.

defs: In a small quantity or degree; not much; slightly; somewhat; -- often with a preceding it.
